name: Dongzhi Festival
traditions: Eating tangyuan (glutinous rice balls) in southern China; jiaozi (dumplings) in northern China; family reunions; ancestral worship.
historicalBackground: Dongzhi has been celebrated since the Han Dynasty as one of the most important solar terms. It marks the return of longer daylight hours.
description: China's Winter Solstice festival, where families gather to eat warming tangyuan and dumplings, celebrating the return of longer days.
